mnemonics: W.A.T.E.R. (Waterborne: A Typhoid, E. coli, Rotavirus)
D.I.S.E.A.S.E. (Diseases In Standing water: Elephantiasis, Schistosomiasis, Arsenicosis)
keyPoints: Waterborne diseases are caused by ingestion of water contaminated with pathogenic microorganisms (e.g., Cholera, Typhoid).
Water-washed diseases are caused by poor hygiene due to water scarcity (e.g., Trachoma, Scabies).
Water-based diseases are caused by parasites that spend part of their life cycle in an aquatic host (e.g., Schistosomiasis).
commonMistakes: Thinking all diseases from water are caused by drinking it; some are from skin contact or insect vectors.
Confusing waterborne (ingestion) with water-based (contact) disease transmission routes.
Assuming that clear water is always safe, ignoring chemical contaminants like arsenic (water-induced).
